the_answer: Your AI stack is already an A-grade. The bullshit you want eliminated isn't a tooling problem; it's an integration problem you happen to be the only person solving.

scores.interpretation: Tools present, plumbing missing. Three connector workflows pulled out of your seat give you back ~7 hrs/week.

wins_top_3.headline: Disconnect the JetPay zombie sign-up feed.
wins_top_3.one_liner: 815 of your 1,149 open deals (71%) sit in pipelines explicitly labeled DO NOT USE.
wins_top_3.ai_mechanism: One-hour HubSpot Workflows audit. Zero code.
wins_top_3.evidence: 142 of 200 sampled open deals sit in pipelines named Jetpay Registration New - DO NOT USE. New records arrived today.

wins_top_3.headline: Build the Granola → Gmail-draft + HubSpot-note loop.
wins_top_3.one_liner: 26 Fathom recordings in 30 days, zero CRM writebacks. The wiki has the playbook; the connectors are wired.
wins_top_3.ai_mechanism: A Claude skill triggered by Fathom recap that drafts the email + writes the CRM note.
wins_top_3.evidence: Four manual post-call recap emails authored within 7 minutes of the Fathom recap landing.

wins_top_3.headline: Replace the calendar-invite-email factory with a one-line generator.
wins_top_3.one_liner: 38 calendar-admin email threads in 30 days, all authored manually, all structurally identical.
wins_top_3.ai_mechanism: A Claude skill that fires the right batch from one line of input.
wins_top_3.evidence: Michael & Son thread on April 30 produced nine separate emails in three hours for one 30-min pre-game.

why_now: Today is the inflection moment. Companies are splitting into two AI plays — use AI to do the same things slightly cheaper, or use AI to do things that were impossible last quarter. The companies pulling ahead aren't cutting headcount. They're running outbound motions no human team could sustain, building knowledge layers that compound, shipping product before competitors notice. Baseline has the connector surface to play that game. What's missing is the wiring.

losing_time.headline: You author every external calendar invite by hand.
losing_time.one_liner: 38 invite-cancel-reschedule threads in 30 days. Michael & Son alone produced nine emails for one 30-minute call.
losing_time.ai_fix: a Claude skill that drafts the right batch from one line of input

losing_time.headline: You re-author post-call emails Fathom already wrote you.
losing_time.one_liner: Fathom recap arrives at 21:34, your manual email goes at 21:41. Same content, different voice.
losing_time.ai_fix: a scheduled task that drafts the follow-up the moment Fathom recap lands

losing_time.headline: Your Esker pipeline report is a 90-min biweekly task that's already a deterministic query.
losing_time.one_liner: Pull HubSpot Esker pipeline, filter to Needs Analysis+, format as ranked list, email.
losing_time.ai_fix: a Cowork scheduled task that runs Monday 8am and drops the formatted draft in your inbox

losing_time.headline: Merchants email you for hardware and account changes that should never touch your inbox.
losing_time.one_liner: Osprey, Sea Air, RVYC, Pacific Backlot — all sent terminal/account requests directly to tyler@.
losing_time.ai_fix: Gmail AI triage that auto-routes hardware/account requests to elsy@ / georgina@

roadmap.title: Quick wins
roadmap.body: Ship the 3 wins above. Plus: a knowledge base on the Drive that every AI tool reads from. Replace stale Notion doctrine with the canonical wiki.

roadmap.title: Skills layer
roadmap.body: Custom skills for your top 5 recurring workflows: pipeline reports, post-call recap drafts, calendar admin, ticket intake, weekly research digests. Your personal cookbook.

roadmap.title: Scheduled tasks
roadmap.body: Skills run on cron. Esker drafts arrive Monday 8am. Daily competitive scan in your inbox at 7am. Weekly pipeline hygiene check. You wake up to drafts, not work.

roadmap.title: Durable agents
roadmap.body: Cloud-deployed agents triggered by real-world events. New ticket → AI-drafted response in 30 sec. New deal stage → CRM enrichment + research brief. Work happens before you sit down.

lanes.name: DIY
lanes.headline: Run /kb-build now
lanes.body: Free, ~20 min. Mining subagents read your connectors and scaffold the wiki. You own everything.

lanes.name: Light-touch
lanes.headline: Have Prescyent build your knowledge base + skills
lanes.body: We build the knowledge base foundation plus a few custom skills mapped to your workflows. Hand it back. Support your team through the first month.

lanes.name: Full
lanes.headline: Engage Prescyent for the complete discovery
lanes.body: Our team interviews leadership. Voice agents interview the rest of the company. Custom plugin built around how you actually run.

path_forward: The audit findings point at one structural pattern: every AI tool at Baseline operates without a persistent context layer accessible to the team. The fix is finishing what you started — a single knowledge base on the Drive that every AI tool reads from, mined from your connectors, kept current automatically.
tyler_brief: Baseline Payments has a v5.0.0 structured knowledge base on the Drive that almost no founder builds. Their AI stack is A-grade. The bottleneck is that every workflow runs through Tyler personally instead of through deterministic automation. Three immediate wins (zombie pipeline disconnect, Granola→Gmail loop, calendar-invite generator) free up ~7 hrs/week. The bigger move is finishing the KB so Marc, JS, and Matt operate from the same canonical source. Founder, ready for Phase 1 plugins.
company_industry: B2B payments processor — Canadian merchant services + payment infrastructure

kb_explainer.what_it_is: A single source on your Google Drive that every AI tool reads from. Karpathy-style — structured pages your team writes once, that compound across every Claude session, every employee, every quarter.
kb_explainer.why_now_for_company: The audit found that every AI workflow at Baseline currently flows through one human (you) instead of through a shared knowledge layer the team can self-serve. A knowledge base directly fixes that. Without it, every workflow on the roadmap above runs cold every time a new session starts.
what_youd_build: Esker pipeline doctrine — so the Monday report draft has context for stage hygiene + Virginie's review pattern.
Customer onboarding patterns — so support ticket triage knows when to escalate to Elsy/Georgina.
Sales playbook — so post-call drafts match your team's voice and competitive positioning.
Calendar admin doctrine — so the invite-generator skill knows your internal vs external attendee patterns.
kb_explainer.how_kb_build_does_it: Mining subagents read your connectors. They distill what's already there into typed pages. Then `/kb-interview` adds the parts that only live in your team's heads — 30-min voice conversations with your senior team that fill the gaps. The whole thing takes about a week.

coverage.records_analyzed: 127 Drive files · 47 Notion pages · 11 deep reads
100+ threads · 41 meetings · 82 chat spaces · 26 Fathom recordings
Catalog scored across rubric · 2 unconnected adjacencies

findings.headline: Two pipelines labeled DO NOT USE hold ~815 of 1,149 open deals (71%).
findings.recommendation: Audit HubSpot Workflows for any create-deal action targeting pipeline IDs 10112334 or 2123176. Disable. Migrate any real records to a clean Onboarding pipeline.

findings.headline: 65% of open Baseline Sales deals have a close date in the past while still open.
findings.recommendation: HubSpot Workflow that nudges the owner with an AI-drafted re-engagement context when closedate passes and stage is open. Two-hour build.

findings.headline: 71% of open Baseline Sales deals have no amount populated. Tracked won revenue reflects fewer than 60% of actual won deals.
findings.recommendation: Require amount on stage change to Proposal. One-time historical enrichment from payment-processor data.

findings.headline: The Baseline Wiki (v5.0.0 on the Drive) is the most AI-ready knowledge artifact in your stack.
findings.recommendation: Keep building. Promote canonical Drive wiki via /wiki:review pass on superseded Notion pages.

findings.headline: 15+ Notion pages carry SUPERSEDED banners but are still searchable. Team is reading stale doctrine.
findings.recommendation: Run /wiki:review to add hard-redirect banners on Notion + one Notion index page linking the nine Drive knowledge files.

findings.headline: 38% of email volume is calendar admin you author manually.
findings.recommendation: Subsumed by Top 3 move #3 — Claude skill replaces the calendar-invite factory.

findings.headline: You are running Fathom AND Granola simultaneously on the same meetings.
findings.recommendation: Pick Granola as primary transcript SOR (semantic search), use Fathom for person/identity enrichment only. Document the SOR split.

findings.headline: Gmail send scope not granted. Current automation ceiling is draft-and-review.
findings.recommendation: This is the right starting point. Use it for two weeks, calibrate trust, then decide whether to grant send for low-risk categories.

findings.headline: Trumpet (digital sales room) is unconnected. Buyer engagement data — the highest-intent signal in B2B payments — is fully siloed.
findings.recommendation: Configure Trumpet's native HubSpot integration. 30-minute setup.

conflicts.summary: Drive wiki says Sandler/NEPQ. Notion superseded pages still surface Challenger framing in search.
conflicts.recommendation: Adopt Drive as canonical; add hard-redirect banners on Notion.

conflicts.summary: Fathom and Granola both running on the same meetings.
conflicts.recommendation: Granola primary for transcripts; Fathom secondary for person/identity.
conflicts.needed_decision: One decision; the SOR split is documented after that.

coverage_gaps.fix: Configure Koncert native HubSpot sync (Matt owns).
Reauthorize the HubSpot MCP with crm.schemas.read and settings.users.read.

open_questions.question: Are the HubSpot email nurture sequences actually live, or documented intent only?
open_questions.recommended_answer: Spend five minutes in HubSpot > Sequences. If documented but not deployed, this is a one-step deployment of an already-written automation.
open_questions.needed_decision: Confirm and either deploy or formally retire the documented sequences.

next_steps_role_aware: Review the Top 3 wins with your senior team this week. The zombie-pipeline fix is the single highest-surprise finding — disconnect before the next pipeline review.
next_steps_connector_aware: Connect Trumpet to HubSpot via the native integration. Buyer engagement data is the highest-intent signal in B2B payments and it's currently siloed.
tan_attribution_footnote: The zero-sum vs positive-sum framing comes from Garry Tan's February 2026 essay on AI strategy bifurcation.

behavioral_history_findings.pattern: User runs /gtm-wizards:call-prep 14 times in 30 days. 11 of 14 sessions ended with manual edits to the AI-drafted deck. Pattern is deterministic with manual finishing — strong scheduled-task candidate.
behavioral_history_findings.evidence: Cowork session history: 14 matching sessions across 30 days, last-30-message tails sampled.

behavioral_history_findings.pattern: Three pipeline-restage sessions in the last 60 days share an identical prompt-pattern (paste deal list + customer-segment doctrine + restage-goal sentence). Indicates a custom skill in disguise.
behavioral_history_findings.evidence: Cowork session history: 3 matching sessions sampled.

behavioral_history_findings.pattern: Two of three Notion-restructure sessions in the last 90 days ended with the user reverting AI-suggested page hierarchy. Implies the AI's structural guess didn't carry the team's mental model — strong KB-builder mining candidate before any further structure work.
behavioral_history_findings.evidence: Cowork session history: 3 sessions sampled, 2 closed with revert pattern.

lane_health.headline: HubSpot connector wasn't reachable
lane_health.impact: GTM/Systems findings are inferred from verbatim pain + industry baselines for sub-10-person merchant services firms. No record-level hygiene metrics computed.
lane_health.fix: Connect HubSpot in your Cowork session settings and re-run /discover for a real read.

lane_health.headline: Google Drive connector was blocked
lane_health.impact: Drive findings are limited to inference. Folder taxonomy, doctrine pages, and voice samples weren't accessible.
lane_health.fix: Connect Google Drive in Cowork settings (or grant the existing connector additional scopes) and re-run /discover.

vocabulary_primer.knowledge_base: A single source on your drive every AI tool reads from.
vocabulary_primer.plugin: A cookbook of capabilities tailored to your company.
vocabulary_primer.skill: A single recipe in that cookbook — one workflow, one trigger.
vocabulary_primer.agent: A recipe that hands itself off to other recipes when the work needs more than one step.
vocabulary_primer.scheduled_task: A recipe that runs on a clock, even when you're asleep.
vocabulary_primer.kicker: Each one removes a kind of toil. The audit picks the ones that hurt most this quarter.

voice_samples.excerpt: Wanted to flag the Q2 number before our Tuesday sync. The pipeline shifted right after the JetPay disconnect — happy to walk through the numbers if useful.
Q1 closed strong on the partnership side. Three things we shipped that compound: the Esker deployment, the migration to Granola, and the wiki rewrite.

comms_patterns.cross_channel_decision_flow: Decisions surface in chat (40%), formalized in calendar invites (30%), confirmed in meeting summaries (30%).

cadence_patterns.pattern: Every Tuesday with Esker partner team

transcript_deep_reads.title: Esker Q1 review
transcript_deep_reads.why_selected: Decision: Esker partner team committed to Q2 deployment timeline
transcript_deep_reads.key_extract: We agreed Tuesday timing works for the deployment kickoff. Tyler to send the team allocation spreadsheet by Friday; Esker to confirm the staging environment by Monday. Q2 deployment locked.

per_entity_summaries.description: B2B payments processor with merchant services + AP/AR automation reseller positioning
Merchant-onboarding product line under Baseline Payments umbrella

resume_trace.follow_up_prompt: You found 815 zombie deals but didn't dig into ownership. Who owns the deals in DO NOT USE pipelines?
resume_trace.refined_finding_summary: Of 815 zombie deals, 612 owned by 3 reps who left in Q4 2025; remainder unassigned. Workflow at risk if any of those reps' contacts are still being routed to.

resume_trace.follow_up_prompt: Your voice_pattern is sparse — pull 5 more sent threads from external counterparties to anchor the formality scoring.
resume_trace.refined_finding_summary: Voice formality confirmed casual across external counterparty threads. Em-dash density 0.4/100w (low). Sign-off pattern consistent first-name-only.

findings.headline: Zombie pipelines hold 815/1149 open deals
findings.recommendation: Audit HubSpot Workflows; disable any create-deal action targeting DO NOT USE pipelines.

findings.headline: 11 connectors visible; Trumpet (digital sales room) unconnected
findings.recommendation: Configure Trumpet's native HubSpot integration. 30-min setup.

findings.headline: User runs /gtm-wizards:call-prep 14 times in 30 days
findings.data_source: Cowork session history, 14 matching sessions sampled at last-30-message tail
findings.recommendation: Convert to a scheduled task that runs Mondays + Wednesdays at 7am.

findings.headline: Baseline's public positioning leads with merchant-services + AP/AR automation reseller — not with Esker partnership.
findings.recommendation: Either elevate Esker partnership to homepage hero (matches internal GTM) OR explicitly position Esker as a back-end capability.
